briefing.title: Market-Leading Sell-Through
briefing.detail: Purse Maison leads all stores with 40 units sold MTD (₱12.21M revenue), more than double the nearest volume competitor Miss Manila Luxe (69 units but at ₱2.62M — far lower ticket), and 4x The Orange Box and The Bag Hub combined.
briefing.action: Maintain current velocity by ensuring top-sold models (Birkin, Kelly, Picotin) are restocked immediately as they clear.

briefing.title: Hermès Birkin Pricing Gap — Risk
briefing.detail: Your lowest Birkin 25 ask is ₱1,288,000 vs. market low of ₱950,000 (+35.6% premium); Birkin 30 lowest ask ₱770,000 vs. market low ₱575,000 (+33.9% premium) — both significantly above the cheapest competition.
briefing.action: Audit the 2 Birkin 25s and 2 Birkin 30s at your floor price — if condition/hardware is not exotic or exceptional, consider trimming to ₱1,100,000 and ₱680,000 respectively to stay competitive without racing to the bottom.

briefing.title: Kelly 28 Overstock vs. Market
briefing.detail: You hold 11 Kelly 28s with a lowest ask of ₱735,000 against a market low of ₱480,000 across 28 live market units — this is the most saturated hero model in the market.
briefing.action: Tier the 11 units: price the 2–3 weakest-condition pieces at ₱580,000–₱620,000 to drive velocity; hold exotic/rare hardware pieces at premium.

briefing.title: Chanel Double Flap Jumbo — Heavy Position
briefing.detail: You hold 23 Jumbo DFLs (largest single-model position in your portfolio) at a lowest ask of ₱188,000, exactly matching the market floor across 36 total live units — a crowded, price-sensitive segment.
briefing.action: Introduce a bundle or styling promotion for Jumbos priced ₱188,000–₱220,000 to accelerate movement; avoid further buying in this size until inventory drops below 15 units.

briefing.title: Inventory Depth vs. Monthly Burn Rate
briefing.detail: At 40 units/month sold and 1,595 units in stock (avg ticket ₱179,548), current inventory represents ~40 months of supply at ₱286.4M — capital is heavily tied up.
briefing.action: Set a 90-day target to reduce inventory by at least 100 units through markdown events and consignment partnerships, prioritizing slow-moving Chanel and Other-brand SKUs.

briefing.title: Luxury Wish Anomaly — Zero Sales
briefing.detail: Luxury Wish has 159 units (₱47.26M inv. value, avg ticket ₱297,233) with 0 sales MTD despite a historically fast 4.9-day avg sell time — suggesting a possible platform outage, pricing freeze, or stock refresh pause.
briefing.action: Monitor Luxury Wish listings daily this week; if they re-activate with aggressive pricing on Chanel (44.8% of mix) or Hermès (38.8%), pre-empt with targeted price adjustments on overlapping SKUs.

alerts: THREAT: Miss Manila Luxe is moving 69 units MTD at avg ticket ₱38,014 — mass-market volume player capturing entry-level buyers; not a direct competitor but signals strong demand at accessible price points in the Manila market.
OPPORTUNITY: Mini Kelly (Kelly 20) — only 4 units live market-wide, your 2 units at ₱1,540,000 vs. market high of ₱1,680,000; scarcity supports holding or nudging price up to ₱1,600,000.

OPPORTUNITY: Constance 18 — your lowest ask (₱404,000) matches the market floor, giving you price leadership; with 10 units in stock and 17 market-wide, consider raising the floor on top 3 condition pieces to ₱450,000+.

THREAT: Shop with K avg ticket ₱333,629 (vs. your ₱179,548) with Hermès Constance and Della Cavalleria in their top sellers — they are capturing the ultra-premium Hermès buyer; ensure your exotic/rare Hermès pieces are actively marketed.
DATA NOTE: Competitor 'sold' figures are inferred from de-listings and may undercount actual sales — treat The Bag Hub (9 units, ₱7.7-day avg) and A&J Luxury velocity as directional signals only.

pricing.suggestion: Your floor of ₱1,288,000 is 35.6% above the market low of ₱950,000; unless the entry piece is exotic leather or rare hardware, reduce floor to ₱1,050,000–₱1,100,000 to convert faster while retaining premium over mass-market competition.
Floor at ₱770,000 sits 33.9% above market low of ₱575,000; reposition weakest-condition unit to ₱650,000–₱680,000 to capture buyers comparing across stores, and hold B-grade and above at ₱750,000+.

Market is saturated (28 live units, low at ₱480,000); create a clear 3-tier: entry pieces at ₱580,000, mid-grade at ₱680,000–₱750,000, and exotic/rare hardware at ₱900,000+ to differentiate and drive volume on the lower tier.

You match the market floor at ₱188,000 across an oversupplied 36-unit market; hold floor but cap new buying — consider a ₱5,000–₱10,000 promotional discount for buyers purchasing 2+ Chanel pieces to clear volume without public markdown.

Your floor at ₱404,000 ties the market low — with 10 units in hand you have pricing power; raise the 2–3 best-condition pieces to ₱460,000–₱500,000 and keep only 1–2 units at ₱404,000 as the traffic-driver SKU.

Scarcity play: only 4 units live market-wide; your ₱1,540,000 floor is below the market high of ₱1,680,000 — test raising 1 unit to ₱1,650,000 given low supply and strong Hermès sell-through at your store this month.
